Motion For Default Judgment And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jul-17-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being set for hearing on Plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Samuel K. Feng; Clerk N Webb; Proceedings Not Reported.

Plaintiff East West Bank is represented by Robert Kaplan, Esq.
Defendant 3100 North First, LLC. not appearing.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff East West Bank, and against Defendant 3100 North First, LLC., as follows:

IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that, pursuant to the terms of that certain Deed of Trust, Assignment of Leases and Rents, Security Agreement, and Fixture Filing dated October 30, 2018 executed by 3100 North First, LLC, as trustor, in favor of First American Title Insurance company; as trustee, for the benefit of the East West Bank, as beneficiary, with respect to the Deed of Trust Property which includes the real property commonly known as 3100 North First Street, San Jose, California which was recorded on October 31, 2018 in the Official Records of Santa Clara County Recorder's Office, as Document No. 24053513 and pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 564b(l) and (11) and Civil Code 2938(c)(l), the Bank was entitled to specific performance and appointment of Receiver against Defendant, and to have the Receiver appointed to take possession and charge to operate the Deed of Trust Property which includes the real property commonly known as 3100 North First Street, San Jose, California as more specifically set forth in the Order Appointing Receiver Ex Parte; Temporary Restraining Order in Aid of Receiver; and Order to Show Cause Re: Confirmation of Appointment of Receiver and Preliminary Injunction in Aid of Receiver entered on December 29, 2023, and the Order Re: Confirmation of Appointment of Receiver and Preliminary Injunction in Aid of Receiver entered on January 30, 2024 (collectively, the "Orders Appointing Receiver") in the above-entitled action. (D505)
